title: Testetest
descrition: Leonardo of Pisa is a very cautious man, and still many months until Christmas, he already bought his Christmas tree. It's a very high tree, higher than the Pisa tower. Leonardo wants to garnish his tree with balls and tree dazzlers. For that, he bought many balls, each one with a diameter between 1 and N. In fact, he bought so many balls that he doesn't know what to do with all of them. In Pisa, each balls has two threads hanging from it where they can stick other balls. In that way we make sure that the balls will not fall off from the tree. Every thread from the balls measure exactly 20 centimeters. As every good Christmas tree, Leonardo's tree has a peak. In it, Leonardo will put a ball of diameter N, since its the most striking. All other balls will be hanged to the peak ball directed or through other balls. Leonardo studied how he needs to hang the balls so that his tree is the most beautiful one from Pisa, and came to the following conclusions: No ball of diameter 1 or 2 can have another ball hanging from it. Every ball of diameter k >= 3 must have two balls hanged: one with diameter k-1 and the other with diameter k-2. There's always space to put all balls that he wants, because his trees are incredibly big. Nevertheless, he feels that his tree it's not the most beautiful one from the city: it's missing the colored light bulbs! Leonardo bought a special strip of bulbs for the tree. The strip has bulbs united by a cable, with 20 centimeters separating each bulb from one another. Each bulb fits perfectly to one ball depending on its size: a bulb of type i can only fit a ball of diameter i, for i = 1, 2, ..., N. If the diameter of the ball is bigger than the size of the bulb, the bulb will not go in, and if its smaller, the bulb will fall. One cannot fit two bulbs in the same ball, and the cable between the lights must be tensioned. This means, in particular, that if between two balls there's no thread then its distance wont be exactly 20 centimeters, so you cannot put two consecutive bulbs between them. Help Leonardo figure out, given the strip of light bulbs and the diameter N of the biggest ball in the tree, if its possible to fit the strip into the tree.
format_input: There are many test cases in the input file. The first line contains two integers N and K (2 <= N, K <= 10^5), representing the maximum diameter of the balls and the amount of bulbs in the strip that he bought, respectively. The second line contains K integers L1, L2, ..., Lk that describe the strip of light bulbs. The i-th integer Li represents the type of the i-th light in the strip (1 <= Li <= N for i = 1, 2, ..., K).
format_output: For each test, if Leonardo can fit the strip of light bulbs into the tree, print S, otherwise print N.
